title: Manipulating the DOM with Refs
description: Focus inputs, measure elements, control media.

slides.title: Imperative DOM Actions 🎮
slides.content:
# Common Use Cases

Once you have a ref pointing to a DOM node, you can call native DOM methods on it.

## Focus Input

```jsx
inputRef.current.focus();
```

## Measure Element Size

```jsx
const rect = divRef.current.getBoundingClientRect();
console.log(rect.width, rect.height);
```

## Control Video/Audio

```jsx
videoRef.current.play();
videoRef.current.pause();
```

These actions are **imperative** – you're telling the element what to do, rather than describing what the UI should look like.

slides.title: Focus on Mount
slides.content: A common pattern: automatically focus an input when a component mounts.

slides.title: Measuring an Element
slides.content: Get the size of an element after it renders:

quizData.question: You can use a ref to call `.focus()` on an input element.
quizData.explanation: Yes, after obtaining the DOM node via ref, you can call its methods.
